lesson.title: Point Estimation
lesson.body: A point estimator is a statistic θ̂ used to estimate an unknown population parameter θ. Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) finds the parameter value that maximizes the likelihood L(θ|x) = ∏f(xᵢ; θ). An estimator is unbiased if E[θ̂] = θ. The Mean Squared Error MSE = Bias² + Variance quantifies total estimation error.

rooms.question: An estimator θ̂ is unbiased if:
rooms.explanation: Unbiasedness means the expected value of the estimator equals the true parameter, regardless of the sample.

rooms.question: For an i.i.d. sample X₁,...,Xₙ from N(μ, σ²), the MLE of μ is:
rooms.explanation: Differentiating the log-likelihood and setting to zero gives the sample mean as the MLE for μ.

rooms.question: The sample variance S² = Σ(Xᵢ − X̄)²/(n−1) is used instead of dividing by n because:
answers: Dividing by n−1 makes S² an unbiased estimator of σ²
Both A and C are correct
rooms.explanation: Both are correct: dividing by n gives a biased estimator E[S²_biased] = (n-1)σ²/n < σ², while dividing by n−1 corrects for this bias.

rooms.question: Order the steps of maximum likelihood estimation:
items: Take log: ℓ(θ) = Σlog f(xᵢ; θ)
Differentiate ℓ(θ) with respect to θ
rooms.explanation: Standard MLE procedure: write likelihood, log-transform, differentiate log-likelihood, solve for the MLE.

rooms.question: MSE of an estimator decomposes as:
rooms.explanation: MSE = E[(θ̂ − θ)²] = Bias² + Variance. This bias-variance tradeoff is fundamental in statistics and machine learning.

boss.name: The Biased Judge
boss.taunt: Your estimate is systematically wrong. Consistency is not accuracy.

questions.question: The MLE of p for X ~ Binomial(n, p) based on a single observation x is:
questions.explanation: L(p) = p^x(1-p)^(n-x). Setting d/dp[log L] = x/p − (n-x)/(1-p) = 0 gives p̂ = x/n.

questions.question: An estimator is consistent if:
answers: It converges in probability to θ as n → ∞
It is unbiased for all n
questions.explanation: Consistency means the estimator converges in probability to the true parameter as sample size grows — a weaker but often easier property to establish than unbiasedness.

questions.question: The Cramér-Rao lower bound states that for any unbiased estimator θ̂:
answers: Var(θ̂) ≥ 1/I(θ) where I(θ) is the Fisher information
questions.explanation: The CRLB gives a lower bound on variance of unbiased estimators. An estimator achieving this bound is called UMVUE (uniformly minimum variance unbiased estimator).
